text: Your friend calls you, upset. They just found out they were passed over for a promotion they were promised, and the job went to the manager's nephew. They are talking about quitting on the spot tomorrow morning.
questions: What do you say to your friend right now?
Your friend asks whether they should confront the manager publicly at the morning meeting. How do you respond?

text: A colleague approaches you at work, visibly stressed. They admit they made an error in a report that has already gone to a client, and they are considering saying nothing and hoping no one notices.
questions: How do you respond to your colleague?
Your colleague says that flagging the error will get them fired because they are on a final warning. What do you say now?

text: Think about a time you received criticism that you initially felt was unfair.
questions: Describe the situation and how you reacted at the time.
Looking back, what did you learn from it, and what would you do differently?

text: Your elderly neighbour, who lives alone, tells you their adult son has been taking money from their account 'to manage things', and they seem confused about how much is left. They ask you not to mention it to anyone because they don't want to cause family trouble.
questions: What do you say to your neighbour?
What, if anything, would you do after this conversation, and why?

text: You are studying with a close friend the night before a major exam. They show you photos of the exam questions, which they say a student from last term sent them. Your friend is on academic probation and will lose their scholarship if they fail.
questions: What would you do in this situation, and why?
Your friend says everyone in the class already has the photos and refusing only hurts you both. How does this change your response, if at all?

text: You work part-time at a pharmacy. You notice a well-liked senior colleague repeatedly leaving early while asking others to punch out their timecard for them. The manager has praised this colleague publicly as a model employee.
questions: What would you do in this situation, and why?
What factors would you consider before deciding whether to involve the manager?

text: A teammate on your intramural team has become withdrawn, missed two games, and posted something online that worried other teammates, though it wasn't explicit. Some teammates want to say something; others feel it's private.
questions: What would you do, and why?
How would you balance your teammate's privacy against the team's concern for their wellbeing?

text: You are volunteering at a clinic. A patient quietly tells you that another volunteer gave them advice about stopping their prescribed medication in favour of a supplement the volunteer sells on the side. The patient asks you not to make a big deal of it.
questions: How would you respond to the patient, and why?
What, if anything, would you do about the other volunteer?

text: You are on a hiring committee for a student club. One applicant is your roommate, who doesn't know you are on the committee. They are qualified, but so are several other applicants. The committee chair hasn't asked about conflicts of interest.
questions: What would you do in this situation, and why?
Describe a time you had to balance a personal relationship against a responsibility to others. What did you learn?

text: You are leading a group project worth 40% of the course grade. One member has contributed almost nothing, but privately tells you they have been dealing with a serious family situation and asks you not to tell the others, who want to report the member to the professor.
questions: What would you do in this situation, and why?
How would you respond to the group members who want to report your teammate?

text: You work at a busy café. A regular customer loudly accuses a new coworker of getting their order wrong on purpose and demands the coworker be fired. You saw the whole exchange: the order slip was ambiguous and the mistake was understandable.
questions: How would you handle this situation in the moment?
What would you say to your coworker afterward, and why?
